article_meta.title: Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 | President Donald Trump

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 | President Donald Trump
SOURCE: Associated Press

ARTICLE:

Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says President Donald Trump's plane, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews about an hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the decision to return was made after takeoff when the crew aboard Air Force One identified "a minor electrical issue" and, out of an abundance of caution, decided to turn around. Things are unloaded from Air Force One after the plane, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Form in Davos, experienced a minor electrical issue after departure, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, and returned to Joint Base Andrews, Md. Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) Members of the media exit Air Force One after the plane, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Form in Davos, experienced a minor electrical issue after departure, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, and returned to Joint Base Andrews, Md. Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) President Donald Trump boards Air Force One for a trip to attend the World Economic Form in Davos, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, at Joint Base Andrews, Md. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 President Donald Trump's plane, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews about an hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the decision to return was made after takeoff when the crew aboard Air Force One identified "a minor electrical issue" and, out of an abundance of caution, decided to turn around.
```json
{
  "summary": "Air Force One, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, returned to Joint Base Andrews approximately one hour after departure on Tuesday evening, January 20, 2026. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that the aircraft's crew identified a minor electrical issue after takeoff and decided to return out of an abundance of caution. The incident occurred during what was intended to be a direct flight to Switzerland for Trump's attendance at the international economic summit. Following the unscheduled return, items were unloaded from the aircraft, and media personnel exited the plane. According to official statements, President Trump was scheduled to board a second aircraft to complete his journey to Davos. The White House characterized the electrical problem as minor, and the precautionary return was presented as a standard safety measure rather than an emergency situation.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Air Force One returned to Joint Base Andrews about one hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening, January 20, 2026",
    "The crew identified a minor electrical issue after takeoff and decided to return as a precautionary measure",
    "President Trump was traveling to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland",
    "White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed the incident and characterized it as a minor issue",
    "President Trump was scheduled to board a second plane to complete the trip to Switzerland"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "White House official statement via press secretary",
    "Associated Press reporting and photography"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Air Force One, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, returned to Joint Base Andrews approximately one hour after departure on Tuesday evening, January 20, 2026. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that the aircraft's crew identified a minor electrical issue after takeoff and decided to return out of an abundance of caution. The incident occurred during what was intended to be a direct flight to Switzerland for Trump's attendance at the international economic summit. Following the unscheduled return, items were unloaded from the aircraft, and media personnel exited the plane. According to official statements, President Trump was scheduled to board a second aircraft to complete his journey to Davos. The White House characterized the electrical problem as minor, and the precautionary return was presented as a standard safety measure rather than an emergency situation.
key_facts: Air Force One returned to Joint Base Andrews about one hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening, January 20, 2026
The crew identified a minor electrical issue after takeoff and decided to return as a precautionary measure
President Trump was traveling to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland
White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed the incident and characterized it as a minor issue
President Trump was scheduled to board a second plane to complete the trip to Switzerland
sources_represented: White House official statement via press secretary
